revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toga Jinja
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = Honden of Toga Shrine | type = | dedication = Ōkuninushi no mikoto | founded = [[Taihō (era)| Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | phone = 0533-93-2001 | website = http://www.togajinja.or.jp/ }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Jinja'''|玉前神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' of Toga Jinja is {{nihongo|'''Ōkuninushi'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}}. ==History== The origins of Toga Jinja are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)| Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze bell, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine is mentioned as the ''ichinomiya'' of Mikawa Province in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]]. However, repeated fires and other disasters over the centuries have destroyed all of the old shrine records and buildings. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|[[ Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|''Kokuhei Shōsha'']]|国幣小社}}, on the lowest rank of the nationally significant shrines. Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. ==See also== {{commonscat }} * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [http://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan&dq=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan&client=firefox-a&pgis=1 ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]]

revision.comment: Gryffindor moved page [[Toga jinja]] to [[Toga Shrine]]

''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==Other websites == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-struct-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Ōkuninushi no mikoto | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)| Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' of Toga Shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}}. ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)| Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze bell, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine is mentioned as the ''ichinomiya'' of Mikawa Province in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]]. However, repeated fires and other disasters over the centuries have destroyed all of the old shrine records and buildings. In the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], Chichibu was listed among the 3rd class of nationally significant shrines or {{nihongo|''Kokuhei Shōsha''|国幣小社}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==Other websites == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-struct-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]]

The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)| Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze bell, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine is mentioned as the ''ichinomiya'' of Mikawa Province in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]]. However, repeated fires and other disasters over the centuries have destroyed all of the old shrine records and buildings. In the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], Chichibu was listed among the 3rd class of nationally significant shrines or {{nihongo|''Kokuhei Shōsha''|国幣小社}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)| Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' of Toga Shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}}. ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)| Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze bell, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine is mentioned as the ''ichinomiya'' of Mikawa Province in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was ranked as a 3rd rank national shrine {{nihongo|''Kokuhei Shōsha''|国幣小社}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==Other websites == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' of Toga Shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}}. ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze bell, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine is mentioned as the ''ichinomiya'' of Mikawa Province in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was ranked as a 3rd rank national shrine {{nihongo|''Kokuhei Shōsha''|国幣小社}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Other websites == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|Kokuhei Shōsha}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[Mikawa Ichinomiya Station] on the [[JR East]] [[Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|Kokuhei Shōsha}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[Mikawa Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|Kokuhei Shōsha}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|Kokuhei Shōsha}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Kosha]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Test]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia24]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine

The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine

The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:ファイル]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine

The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[category:Mikawa Province]] [[category:Ichinomiya]] [[category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}
