The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Toga Shrine]]{{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Toga Shrine]]{{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Toga Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Toga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Toga Shrine]]{{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|burial mound]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine

It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} 砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} 砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[sn:Toga Shrine]] [[ja:Toga Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[sn:Toga Shrine]] [[ja:Toga Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine

The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons Category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[sn:Toga Shrine]] [[ja:Toga Shrine]]

The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[sn:Toga Shrine]] [[ja:Toga Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[sn:Toga Shrine]] [[ja:Toga Shrine]]

The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[sn:Toga Shrine]] [[ja:Toga Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|51.59|N|137|25|16.38|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ōkuninushi |Ōkuninushi no mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[Yayoi period]] ''[[dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[koku]]'' by the [[Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd rank|国幣小社}|[[Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[sn:Toga Shrine]] [[ja:Toga Shrine]]
