[[arz:ضريح توجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3530344]] Wikidata name: Toga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[:en:Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[:en:Sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] ''[[:en:Dōtaku|Dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' by the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[arz:ضريح توجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3530344]] Wikidata name: Toga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[:en:Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[:en:Sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] ''[[:en:Dōtaku|Dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' by the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}}

The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[arz:ضريح توجا]][[commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3530344]] Wikidata name: Toga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[:en:Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[:en:Sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] ''[[:en:Dōtaku|Dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' by the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' by the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[arz:ضريح توجا]][[commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3530344]] Wikidata name: Toga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[:en:Toyokawa, Aichi|Toyokawa]] in eastern [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a [[:en:Sacred mountains|sacred mountain]] considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō period]] (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] ''[[:en:Dōtaku|Dōtaku]]'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' by the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was battleground between the forces of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' by the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.[[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Iida_Line|Iida Line]] ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

[[arz:ضريح توجا]][[co:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[co:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3530344]] Wikidata name: Toga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the city of {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|en|Toyokawa, Aichi|WD=Q389495|lt=Toyokawa}} in eastern {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|WD=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=sacred mountain}} considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. revision.text:
[[arz:ضريح توجا]][[co:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[co:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3530344]] Wikidata name: Toga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the city of {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|en|Toyokawa, Aichi|WD=Q389495|lt=Toyokawa}} in eastern {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|WD=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=sacred mountain}} considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the city of {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|en|Toyokawa, Aichi|WD=Q389495|lt=Toyokawa}} in eastern {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|WD=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=sacred mountain}} considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded_by = | established = {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the city of {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|en|Toyokawa, Aichi|WD=Q389495|lt=Toyokawa}} in eastern {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|WD=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=sacred mountain}} considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

The shrine claims to have been founded in the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine claims to have been founded in the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period}} (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku}}'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station|1=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line|1=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

[[arz:ضريح توجا]][[co:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[co:commons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Toga Shrine]][[en:Toga Shrine]][[fr:Toga-jinja]][[ja:砥鹿神社]][[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | founded_by = | established = {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period|1=Taihō (era)}} (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the city of {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|en|Toyokawa, Aichi|WD=Q389495|lt=Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} in eastern {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|WD=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province|1=Mikawa Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=sacred mountain|1=Sacred mountains}} considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period|1=Taihō (era)}} (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku|1=Dōtaku}}'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station|1=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line|1=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station|1=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line|1=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Toga Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح توجا]] {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} [[fr:Toga-jinja]] [[ja:砥鹿神社]] [[zh:砥鹿神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3530344}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Toga Shrine
砥鹿神社 | image = File:Toga Shrine (main hall 1).jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Toga Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.84766389|N|137.42121667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Toga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | founded_by = | established = {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period|1=Taihō (era)}} (701-704 AD) | date_destroyed = | location = Nishigakiuchi, Ichinomiya-cho, Toyokawa-shi, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 3-5 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Toyokawa|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Toga Shrine'''|砥鹿神社|Toga Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the city of {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|en|Toyokawa, Aichi|WD=Q389495|lt=Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} in eastern {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|WD=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province|1=Mikawa Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually from May 3 to May 5. Located on the borderland of Aichi with {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, the summit of [[Mount Hongū]] {{convert|782|m|ft}} is a {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=sacred mountain|1=Sacred mountains}} considered to be within the precincts of the shrine, and has a subsidiary chapel.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Toga Shrine is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''|玉埼神|Ōkuninushi-no-mikoto}} the god of nation-building, agriculture, medicine, and protective magic.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The origins of Toga Shrine are unknown. The shrine claims to have been founded in the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō period|1=Taihō (era)}} (701-704 AD) by [[:en:Emperor Mommu|Emperor Mommu]]. It is located in an area of eastern Mikawa with a favorable climate, which has been settled since at least the [[Jōmon period]]. One of the treasures of the shrine is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} ''{{ill|Dōtaku|en|Dōtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=Dōtaku|1=Dōtaku}}'' bronze ritual object, possibly recovered from a [[kofun|Kofun]] in the area. The shrine first appears in historical documentation in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 850 and subsequently in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 864. The shrine is mentioned as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Mikawa Province in the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, it was battleground between the forces of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} and the [[imagawa clan]]. It was given a small stipend of 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' by the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} [[Tokugawa shogunate]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} after the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the Toga Shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社}|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. in the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is a five-minute walk from {{ill|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|en|Mikawa-Ichinomiya_Station|WD=Q772219|lt=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station|1=Mikawa-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Iida_Line|en|Iida_Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line|1=Iida Line}} ==Gallery== Toga-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|''Haiden'' Toga-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Toga Shrine (main gate).jpg|Main gate Mount-Hongu-6.jpg|Oku-no-miya on Mount Hongu ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links == {{Commons category-inline|Toga Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://www.togajinja.or.jp/}} *[https://www.aichi-now.jp/spots/detail/1959/ Aichi Prefecture official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.toyokawa.lg.jp/smph/saijibunka/rekishi/rekishikaido/inarijinja/togajinja.html Toyokawa city official homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{Commons category|Toga Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q3530344|arz|ضريح توجا|co|commons:Category:Toga Shrine|en|Toga Shrine|fr|Toga-jinja|ja|砥鹿神社|zh|砥鹿神社}} [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyokawa, Aichi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
